title: Indonesian activist Andrie Yunus attacked with acid | ABC NEWS
description: An Indonesian human rights activist has been attacked with acid in Jakarta, leaving him with burns to 24% of his body.  Andrie Yunus, a deputy coordinator with the rights group Commission for Missing Persons and Victims of Violence (KontraS) was attacked by two men on a motorcycle shortly after recording a podcast criticising the military’s expanding role in civilian affairs.  More than 170 civil society groups have condemned the attack, while the UN human rights chief described it as a “horrific” act of violence.  A parliamentary human rights commission said the attack undermines President Prabowo Subianto’s commitment to human rights, while Indonesia’s law minister called it “an attack on democracy itself.”  Police say they are investigating and reviewing CCTV footage to identify the perpetrators. title: Millions of home borrowers face more pain as costs rise at the pump | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: Mortgage borrowers are facing the pressure of higher prices at the bowser and rising loan repayments. According to research from Roy Morgan, the latest interest rate rise from the Reserve Bank could leave more than 1.4 million home borrowers at risk of mortgage stress once the rate increases are passed through. The Commonwealth Bank, ANZ, Westpac and NAB have announced they'll pass on the rate hike in full by the end of the month. Calls to financial counsellors at the National Debt Helpline have increased to levels not seen in six years.  Dominique Meyrick from Financial Counselling Australia is urging Australians facing tough situations to get on top of their debt faster.  She says, "that might mean calling up your creditors, the bank or the telco or energy company and explaining your situation and asking for a hardship arrangement."  Nab chief economist Sally Auld expects the RBA to lift interest rates again in May but if oil prices continue rising and impact the global economy, she sees a scenario where the central bank suddenly pivots.  She said they will go from a world in which they're worried about inflation and the possibility of having to take rates higher to one where they think the hit to growth is going to be big enough that it transmits from a global recession to Australia. In this case, she said they may need to reverse course. #ABCBusiness  Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE Read more here: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-03-18/asx-markets-business-live-news-march-18/106466908  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: More sellers turning a profit than at any other time in the past 15 years | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: More sellers of residential houses and units in capital cities are turning a profit than at any other time in the past 15 years. That's according to listings agency, Domain, which says extraordinary capital growth is providing a buffer against rising interest rates and inflation. Chief of research and economics at Domain, Nicola Powell says the profits are often in the hundreds of thousands of dollars as house prices rise.  "We are seeing that people are staying in their homes for longer, so that does mean they're reaping the benefits from multiple property price cycles, and we're really seeing that when they go to resell that property, it's (the profit is) hundreds of thousands of dollars," she says. She says the people benefitting the most are those selling to downsize. "When you think about 70 per cent of Australia's household wealth is tied to the value of our homes, when you look at that wealth creation, it's really understandable why we can see the increase of the banker mum and dad and that kind of intergenerational wealth transfer," she says. While Ms Powell says this data shows the people holding onto their homes are likely to be shielded from the impacts of higher interest rates and inflation, the most vulnerable cohort are first home buyers. #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE Read more here: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-03-18/asx-markets-business-live-news-march-18/106466908  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Israel claims deaths of two Iranian officials, Trump says he no longer needs NATO help | ABC NEWS
description: Israel has claimed to have killed Iran's security chief, Ali Larijani, widely seen as one of the most powerful figures in the country, along with General Gholam Reza Soleimani, the head of the Revolutionary Guard's all-volunteer Basij force. Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump has listed Australia among the countries the US does not need to support it in its campaign against Iran. In a post on his Truth Social account, the President described the relationship between the US and its allies, including NATO, as a "one-way street" in which "we will protect them, but they will do nothing for us". title: Seeking shelter from a missile strike in Lebanon | ABC NEWS
description: The shell of an unfinished council building, in the hills overlooking southern Beirut, is alive with activity.  Not from tradespeople, but with children playing football as their anxious parents watch on.  World Vision is supervising the site in Choueifat, commandeering the building from the local municipality and providing tarpaulins and mattresses for desperate people fleeing the violence elsewhere.  Matthew Doran reports from Choueifat.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad.
